\needspace{4\baselineskip}
\section{Classic French Toast}
\recipemeta{4 servings · 5 minutes prep · 15 minutes cook}
\recipedescription{Perfectly golden, custardy French toast with a hint of vanilla and cinnamon.}
\subsection*{Ingredients}
\begin{ingredients}
\item \textbf{eggs} -- 4
\item \textbf{whole milk} -- 1 cup
\item \textbf{vanilla extract} -- 1 tsp
\item \textbf{ground cinnamon} -- 1/2 tsp
\item \textbf{salt} -- 1/4 tsp
\item \textbf{butter} -- 2 tbsp
\item \textbf{bread} -- 8 slices, day-old
\item \textbf{maple syrup}
\item \textbf{powdered sugar}
\end{ingredients}
\subsection*{Instructions}
\begin{enumerate}
\item In a shallow dish or pie plate, whisk together eggs, whole milk, vanilla extract, ground cinnamon, and salt.
\item Heat a griddle or large skillet over medium heat. Add butter and let it melt.
\item Dip bread into the egg mixture, letting each side soak for 3 seconds. Don't oversoak or the bread will fall apart.
\item Place soaked bread on the hot griddle and cook for 3 minutes per side, until golden brown and cooked through.
\item Serve hot with maple syrup and powdered sugar for dusting, if desired.
\end{enumerate}
\recipeattribution{Recipe by King Arthur Baking}
\vspace{3\baselineskip}
